page_content:
Prerequisite: Permission of ARCH-Architecture Program.

ARCH170 Design Thinking and Architecture (3 Credits)

Examines conceptual, perceptual, behavioral, and technical aspects of the built environment, and methods of analysis, problem-solving, and design implementation.

ARCH171 Design Thinking and Making in Architecture (3 Credits) Examines iterative design processes and critical thinking skills through active learning and design thinking methodologies to solve problems

and apply design as a lens of inquiry and exploration. Students will understand Design Thinking through interactive and experiential learning. Restriction: Must be a major in ARCH-Architecture Program; and must be a freshman; or have permission of the School of Architecture, Planning and Preservation.
